title: Chanekia misantlae

Chanekia misantlae's precise classification within the Apocynaceae family is still a subject of scientific investigation.

intro: Chanekia misantlae, also known as Chanca Piedra, is a plant species that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including kidney stones and liver problems, due to its potential to reduce inflammation and promote urination. The bioactive constituents of Chanekia misantlae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Chanca Piedra is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support kidney and liver health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
